0.q: The two durum wheat cDNAs encoding for Na+/H+ antiporter (TdSOS1) and its hyperactive form (TdSOS1D972) were reported in Feki et al. (2011). The activation mechanism of durum wheat TdSOS1 protein involves the phosphorylation and inactivation of an auto-inhibitory domain.
SOS1 is the direct target of SOS signaling pathway, which contains two other components SOS2 and SOS3.

The Na?/H? antiporter SOS1 localized to the plasma membrane... in Arabidopsis three salt overly sensitive genes SOS1, SOS2 and SOS3 have been found to function in a common pathway; TdSOS1 shared high homology with SOS1-like antiporters and is activated by SOS2/SOS3.

1.q: TdAnn6 overexpression positively regulates antioxidant activities in the TdAnn6 lines... the activities of SOD, CAT, and POD were significantly higher in TdAnn6 plants under salt/osmotic stress, with lower H2O2 and MDA levels.

2.q: Transgenic TdNFY-YA2A-1-overexpressing tobacco lines exhibited enhanced tolerance to both salt and osmotic stress...correlated with a reduction in oxidative damage and the upregulation of several stress-responsive genes involved in antioxidant defense.

3.q: salt tolerance of transgenic Arabidopsis plants overexpressing durum wheat dehydrin (DHN-5) was closely related to the activation of the proline metabolism enzyme (P5CS)

4.q: salt tolerance of Arabidopsis transgenic plants was accompanied by an excellent activation of antioxidant enzymes like catalase (CAT), superoxide dismutase (SOD) and peroxide dismutase (POD) and generation of a lower level of hydrogen peroxide

5.q: These data suggested that TdSOS1 plays a crucial role in response to oxidative stress. Transgenic plants showed significant oxidative stress tolerance, with higher activities of SOD, CAT, and POD in the transgenic plants under oxidative stress.

6.q: TdRL1 OE seedlings showed decreased H2O2 content and increased CAT and SOD activities under salt stress; TdRL1 over-expression conferred salt stress tolerance and alleviated ROS accumulation.

7.q: The genomic sequences of the two RR-TZF genes TdTZF1-A and TdTZF1-B were isolated in durum wheat and assigned to chromosomes 3A and 3B, respectively.

8.q: TIFY11a was specifically induced after salt treatment. Transgenic lines over-expressing TdTIFY11a showed higher germination and growth rates under high salinity conditions, compared to wild type plants.

10.q: activated TdSOS1 decreased the net uptake of Na? after 30 min compared to TdSOS1 alone... produced by the activation of the Na? efﬂux.
SOS1 is the direct target of SOS signaling pathway, which contains two other components SOS2 and SOS3.

11.q: TdHKT1;4–1 partially complements the salt sensitivity phenotype of the athkt1 transgenic lines... both transgenic lines SH3 and SH5 restored the salt stress tolerance comparable to the level observed in Wt plants.

12.q: Under salt stress, TdSOS1D972 transgenic lines showed greater water retention capacity and retained low Na+ and high K+ in their shoots and roots.

13.q: overexpression of TdAnn12 in transgenic tobacco improves stress tolerance through the removal of ROS.

14.q: Mutant combinations lacking functional HYD2 homoeologs... showed decreased levels of neoxanthin and violaxanthin (xanthophylls derived from β-carotene) and an increase in β-carotene in roots.

15.q: the durum wheat TdHKT1;4–1 acts as a Na+-selective transporter ... it contributes to salt stress tolerance
